concerns.text: T016 'Implement compute_entropy CLI command... to read CSV, apply entropy functions, and write enriched CSV' is ambiguous regarding the join with logS/logP. FR-004 requires joining with experimental labels. The task does not specify if the input CSV for T016 must already contain logS/logP or if the task must perform the join. Without this, the 'Independent Test' (output CSV with entropy columns) cannot be deterministically executed if the input lacks metadata.

T018 'Add sensitivity analysis logic for Ridge Regression alpha sweep' is misplaced in User Story 1 (Entropy Computation). FR-011 and the Plan define this as part of Model Evaluation (US2). Implementing this in US1 creates a dependency on model training logic that does not exist yet, making the task unexecutable in isolation.

T026 'Implement JSON report generation... with Bonferroni-corrected p-values' focuses on formatting. It does not explicitly mandate the implementation of the Bonferroni correction logic (calculating adjusted alpha or p-values) within the task. The spec FR-010 requires the *application* of the correction. A task to 'generate report' could be executed by writing static JSON without the statistical logic, failing the requirement.

T035 'Update code/entropy.py and docs/data-model.md to explicitly document...' is too coarse. It combines code updates and documentation updates into one task. The atomizer will split this, but for immediate executability, these should be separate tasks: one for code comments/docstrings and one for the markdown file update.

T036 'Add a section to docs/research.md justifying why...' is a writing task that depends on the outcome of the analysis (correlation results). It is not self-contained for an implementer to execute *before* the model is run. This task should be rephrased to 'Draft the justification section template' or moved to post-analysis.

T013 'Integration test for 10k molecule processing time limit (≤30 min)' is not self-contained. It assumes the existence of a specific 10k molecule dataset in the test environment. The task must specify the source of this test data (e.g., 'using a synthetic dataset generated by...') or the test will fail to execute deterministically.
